and shall, also , cause to be posted on the front door
of the Courthouse a similar notice . If the land , in
any drainage district sought to be assessed , lies in
more than one county , a copy of said assessment roll
shall be filed with the Clerk of the County Court of
each county wherein lies said land or any part
thereof and notice of the hearing thereon shall be
given in the manner and form herein provided in
each county wherein any of the said land lies . If
no exceptions are filed to said assessment roll on or
before the date set for the hearing thereon , same
shall be confirmed by the Court . Any land owner ,
whose lands are sought to be assessed , may file ex-
ceptions to said assessment roll or any part thereof,
within the time aforesaid . If any such land owner
shall file exceptions to said assessment roll , the
Court shall hear same in a summary way , and may
either quash , confirm or modify said assessment
roll . The order of the Court quashing , confirming
or modifying said assessment roll shall be a final
order and may be appealed from in the same man-
ner as from orders confirming the preliminary re-
port of the viewers or their final report . Assess-
ments made pursuant hereto shall have the same
force and effect and be collected in the same manner
as original assessments .
§ 2. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Approved March 19, 1928 .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 5
CHAPTER 2 .
AN ACT providing for the quarantine and control of the
European Corn Borer ; imposing certain powers and duties on
the Department of Agriculture ; providing penalties ; providing
an appropriation for carrying out the provisions of the same .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in order to protect the agricultural
and horticultural crops of the Commonwealth from
the ravages of the European corn borer (Pyrausta
nubilaslis ) that may have been or may hereafter be
within the Commonwealth of the State which would
seriously and destructively infest any area in the
State in which it may be found , therefore , the De-
partment of Agriculture shall adopt and carry out
such control measures as are deemed advisable , and
may cooperate with the United States Department
of Agriculture in the control of this pest .
§ 2. The Department of Agriculture is author-
ized to promulgate quarantines and quarantine re-
strictions covering areas within the State affected
by the said pest and areas within the State adjacent
thereto , and may adopt , issue , and enforce rules and
regulations supplemental to such quarantines for
the control of this pest . Under such quarantine, the
Department of Agriculture or its authorized agents.
may prohibit and prevent the movement within the
State without inspection , or the shipment or trans-
portation within the State , of any agricultural or
horticultural product , or any other material of any
character whatsoever , capable of carrying this pest
in any living state of its development ; and , in the
enforcement of such quarantine , may intercept ,
stop , and detain for official inspection any person ,
car , vessel , boat , truck , automobile , aircraft , wagon ,
or other vehicles or carriers whether air, land or
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1928, p. 56

§ 4. Any person , copartnership , association , or

conviction before any mayor , magistrate , or justice

and in case of non -payment of said fine , shall be im-
prisoned in the county jail for a period not exceed-
ing five days : Provided that any person so convicted
shall have the right of appeal , as in other cases of

pany for the consideration of the maintenance and operation

road Company , at the request of the Kentucky State Board of
Agriculture , constructed a track on the property of the State
Board of Agriculture in Louisville , Kentucky , to serve the
Kentucky State Fair . The said track was constructed pur-
suant to a promise on the part of the State Board of Agricul-
ture to convey the necessary land to the Kentucky & Indiana
Terminal Railroad Company required in the construction ,
maintenance and operation of said track . The demands of
the Kentucky State Board of Agriculture for railroad terminal
facilities on its property in Louisville have increased and the
Kentucky & Indiana Terminal Railroad Company has con-
sented to construct , maintain and operate the said terminal
facilities if sufficient land is conveyed to it by the Kentucky

CHAPTER 7 .
AN ACT providing that money appropriated by Congress under
the "forest reserve act " shall be paid to the State Auditor ;
duty of county treasurer in regard to forest reserve lands ;

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That all money or moneys which shall have
already been paid , or which may hereafter be paid
to the State of Kentucky under an act of Congress
approved May twenty -third , nineteen hundred and
eight , and arising from any National " forest re-
serve " created in the State by the Federal Govern-
ment , shall be paid over to the Auditor of Public
Accounts , and be turned into the State treasury , to
be distributed and disposed of as provided in the
following sections .
§ 2. The treasurer of each county in which there
is situated any land owned by the United States
Government and known as " forest reserve " shall
ascertain the area of said land so situated in his
county , and the " reserve " in which the same is lo-
cated , and make report thereof to the Auditor of
Public Accounts . Upon receipt of such information
from the county treasurer , the Auditor of Public
Accounts shall apportion the amount received by
him from the " reserve " in which the said land is
located among the counties in which the said " for-
est reserve " is located , according to the area in each
county ; or if all of the fund received from one reser-
vation lies in one county , it shall all be apportioned
to that county and the Auditor of Public Accounts
shall draw his warrant on the State treasury in
favor of the treasurer of each county for the
amount apportioned to said county , and such trea-
surer shall receive the same and place the funds to

thereof ; prescribing the methods of expenditure , the
assessment and collection of costs and expenses
thereof ; providing for the appointment of commis-
sioners and prescribing their duties and providing
for the payment of officers and employees . The is-
suing and selling of district bonds with which to se-
cure funds to defray the costs and expenses of any
such improvements , repealing an act entitled an Act
" providing for the creation of water districts ,
etc. ," approved March twenty-five, one thousand
nine hundred and twenty-four , which was passed
and approved March twenty- seven , one thousand
nine hundred and twenty -six be and the same is
hereby amended and re -enacted , so that said sec-
tion when amended and re -enacted shall read as
follows :
Section 2. After the county judge shall have
made an order creating said water district , he shall
appoint three commissioners , residents of the water
district , to be known as water district commission-
ers . One of the said commissioners shall be ap-
pointed for the term of two years , one for the term
of three years , and one for the term of four years .
Upon the expiration of the term of office of each of
these commissioners the county judge shall appoint
a commissioner to succeed him to serve for the term
of four years . Said commissioners shall receive a
salary not to exceed two hundred dollars per year to
be fixed by the county judge , and to be paid out of
the treasury of the water district fund .
§ 2. All acts in conflict herewith , are to the ex-
tent of such conflict hereby repealed .
Approved March 20 , 1928 .

and eighteen .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Act creating the office of District Detec-

inserting in lieu thereof one thousand eight hundred
dollars in the second line of section four and by
striking out one thousand eight hundred dollars and
inserting in lieu thereof two thousand four hundred
dollars in the third line of section four of said Act ,
so that said Act as amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows :

having therein cities of the second class , in which
there is located a Circuit Court of continuous ses-

Detective to the Commonwealth's Attorney . Said

entries.ocr_text:
ACTS OF THE General ASSEMBLY 47
VETO MESSAGE
HOUSE BILL NO . 746
It is necessary for the State of Kentucky to live within its
income ; the Constitution requires it . The Budget Commission
undertook to distribute the limited funds of the State to its
several schools , institutions and agencies according to their
needs . When so distributed there would be no deficit , but the
General Assembly amended the budget bill by increasing the
amount of items in some instances and by putting in new items
in other cases , so as to augment the total several hundred thou-
sand dollars without making any provision for the raising of
this money . If the bill , as passed , should be approved the State
would be thrown in debt in a large sum . Everyone realizes that
most of the items thus added by the General Assembly were for
a good purpose and in almost every instance deserving . But
the State cannot spend money that it does not have . It must
cut according to its cloth just as a private citizen must do in
the operation of his business . Had the General Assembly levied
a tax sufficient to raise the revenue necessary for the payment
of these several different items to the budget , as submitted by
the Budget Commission , I would have felt warranted in approv-
ing most all of these items , but in the absence of such levy and
provision to meet the expense , some of these items must be and
are vetoed .
The items disapproved are given herein below :
Subsection (f ) of Section 30 , relating to the State Board of
Health and reading , "For Department of Bacteriology and
Epidermiology, the sum of five thousand and four hundred dol-
lars ( $5,400.00 ) for the increase of salaries of employees of such
department ." This item as originally proposed by the Budget
Commission was for three thousand dollars ( $3,000.00 ) , but was
increased by amendment to five thousand and four hundred dol-
lars ( $5,400.00 ) . I disapprove the increase of two thousand , four
hundred dollars ( $2,400.00 ) . But if the executive has power only
to veto an entire item and not merely that added by an amend-
ment to the budget bill , then the whole of this item is disap-
proved .
Item ( i ) of subsection (i ) of Section 30 , reading , " Repairs to
old building , roof, toilets and baths , etc. , ten thousand dollars
($10,000.00 ) ." This item is disapproved and stricken from the
appropriation .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1928, p. 100

have had but little help from the State during their existence ,

entries.ocr_text:
ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 75
1
VETO MESSAGE
HOUSE BILL NO . 747
It is necessary for the State of Kentucky to live within its
income ; the Constitution requires it . The Budget Commission
undertook to distribute the limited funds of the State to its
several schools , institutions and agencies according to their
needs . When so distributed there would be no deficit , but the
General Assembly amended the budget bill by increasing the
amount of items in some instances and by putting in new items
in other cases so as to augment the total several hundred thou-
sand dollars without making any provision for the raising of
this money . If the bill as passed should be approved , the State
would be thrown in debt in a large sum . Everyone realizes that
most of the items thus added by the General Assembly were for
a good purpose and in almost every instance deserving . But
the State cannot spend money that it does not have . It must
cut according to its cloth just as a private citizen must do in
the operation of his business . Had the General Assembly levied
a tax sufficient to raise the revenue necessary for the payment
of these several different items to the budget as submitted by
the budget commission , I would have felt warranted in approv-
ing most all of these items , but in the absence of such levy and
provision to meet the expenses , some of these items must be
and are vetoed .
The items disapproved are given herein below :
Subsection ( e ) of Section 30 , relating to the State Board of
Health and reading , "For Department of Bacteriology and
Epidermiology , the sum of five thousand and four hundred dol-
lars ( $5,400.00 ) for the increase of salaries of employes of such
department ." This item as originally proposed by the Budget
Commission was for three thousand dollars ( $3,000.00 ) , but was
increased by amendment to five thousand and four hundred dol-
lars ( $5,400.00 ) . I disapprove the increase of two thousand four
hundred dollars ( $ 2,400.00 ) . But if the executive has power only
to veto an entire item and not merely that added by an amend-
ment to the budget bill , then the whole of this item is disap-
proved .
In subsection ( e ) of Section 30 there is also this provision ,
"for infancy , maternity and child health , forty -seven thousand
dollars ($ 47,000.00 ) for the year 1929 to 1930. " This item is dis-
approved in toto and stricken from the appropriation .

CHAPTER 14 .
AN ACT to regulate the business of brokers , and prescribing
penalties for the violation of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any broker who , knowing that he is insol-
vent , accepts or receives or consents or assents to
the receipt from a customer ignorant of such brok-
er's insolvency , deposits of money , stocks , bonds , se-
curities , or other things of value belonging to the
customer , to be used as margin for the purchase of
stocks , bonds , grain , produce , or cotton , otherwise
than in payment of or security for an existing in-
debtedness , and who thereby causes the customer to
lose in whole or in part such money , stocks , bonds ,
securities , or other things of value , shall be guilty
of a felony , and upon conviction shall be punished
by confinement in the penitentiary for not less than
one nor more than ten years .
§ 2. Any broker who , having in his possession ,
for safe -keeping , or otherwise , money , stocks , bonds ,
securities , or other things of value belonging to a
customer , without having any lien thereon or any
special property therein , pledges or disposes
thereof without such customer's consent , shall be
guilty of a felony and upon conviction shall be pun-
ished by confinement in the penitentiary for not less
than one nor more than ten years .
§ 3. A broker , within the meaning of the fore-
going sections , shall be any person , firm, or corpora-
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1928, p. 131

ernor or the Budget Commission ; the board may apportion

between the various institutions in such manner as the in-
terest of such institutions require ; that the appropriation
made for the Board of Charities and Corrections shall be pay-
able by warrant drawn by the Auditor monthly for such sums
of money as are necessary to operate the institutions under
its control, including salaries to all officers and employees .
The order shall be signed by the Commissioner of Public In-
stitutions and approved by the chairman of the board ; pre-
scribing the duty of the Auditor ; providing that the super-

86 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ings and grounds , and for any neglect or refusal on the part
of members of said board they shall be subject to a penalty
of two hundred and fifty dollars ( $250.00 ) ; that said board is
authorized to keep the buildings , furniture and other property
under their control insured ; that they are authorized to cancel
at any time , or cause to be cancelled , any insurance in their
judgment ; that the board may issue license to charitable
organizations supported in whole or in part by appropriations
from the State Treasury ; that no such organizations or repre-
sentative thereof shall solicit outside the county in which it is
located ; that any person violating this provision may be fined
in any sum not less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) nor more than
two hundred dollars ( $200.00 ) ; that the board is vested with
the power of eminent domain and the procedure to condemn
real estate is the same as condemnation proceedings for rail-
road purposes ; that the University of Kentucky shall , at the
request of the Board of Charities and Corrections , examine
and report on any lands or soils which the board may wish
to investigate , and to advise said board of the quality and
character of soil , water supply , sanitation , agricultural and
engineering problems . The services rendered by said Uni-
versity shall be free , but it shall be entitled to receive its
necessary expenses in making said investigations , and said
Board of Charities and Corrections shall make such rules and
regulations as are necessary for the management and control
of the institutions named in this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Powers and Duties of Board . The State
Board of Charities and Corrections is hereby vested
with authority to and shall , except as otherwise
hereinafter provided , exercise all the powers and
privileges and discharge all the duties connected
with or necessary to carry out the purposes for
which said board was created .
2. Corporate Powers . The board shall be a
body politic and corporate and have the powers to
sue and be sued , contract and be contracted with ,
and is invested with all the powers necessary to
carry into full effect the purposes of its establish-
ment .

§ 3. Institutions Under Board of Charities and
Corrections . The institutions hereby placed under
the control and management of the State Board of
Charities and Corrections are nine in number and
are as follows :
State Reformatory at Frankfort , Ky .
State Penitentiary at Eddyville , Ky .
Houses of Reform , Greendale , Ky .
Eastern State Hospital at Lexington , Ky .
Central State Hospital at Lakeland , Ky .
State Hospital for the Criminally Insane , Lake-
land , Ky .
Western State Hospital at Hopkinsville , Ky.
Feeble -Minded Institute at Frankfort , Ky .
House of Reform for Girls in Shelby County , Ky.
§ 4. Regulations Governing Institutions ; Politi-
cal Activity Prohibited ; Penalty . The board shall
make rules and regulations for the strictly non-
partisan management of the institutions under its
control . Any member or employee of the board, or
any officer or employee of any institution under its
control , who shall engage in political activity or who
shall in any manner contribute , or cause to be con-
tributed , money or any other thing of value to any.
person for election purposes , or who shall influence
or attempt to influence legislation through the in-
strumentality of his official position , shall be re-
moved from his office or position by the board in
case of an officer or employee , and by the governor
in case of a member of the board . Any person or
persons wilfully violating the provisions of this sec-
tion shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and
shall be fined in any sum not less than fifty ( $ 50.00 )
nor more than one thousand dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) .

§ 12. Patients , Record of ; Case Histories ; Acci-
dent Book . The superintendent of each institution
for the insane or feeble -minded shall cause to be
kept a register of all patients , showing their names ,
ages , residences , dates of reception and discharge or
death , and by whose authority received or dis-
charged . He shall cause to be kept a case book or
record in which shall be entered a history of the case
of each patient received into the asylum , as far as it
is possible to make out such history from creditable
information ; also the condition at time of admission ,
together with a detailed statement of medical treat-
ment ; an " accident book " in which shall be recorded
a statement of all accidents , whether serious or
slight , to any of the patients of said asylum , whether
self-inflicted or occurring otherwise , together with
a statement of the causes of such accidents or in-
juries ; and also the names , if any , of the persons
who have been witnesses thereof . Said record shall
be open to inspection of the board of charities and
corrections at all times and shall be filed and kept
among the records of the institutions .

stitution .
§ 14. Receiver ; Duties . It shall be the duty of
the receiver to receive all goods and supplies of any
and all kinds purchased for the institution , take
charge of them , see that they correspond with the
bills accompanying them, in character , quality and
quantity ; weigh or measure the same and distribute
them to each and every department of the institution
as the superintendent may direct by written order ;
and , in a book for the purpose , open an account with
each and every department , charging therein cost
price for all goods so received and distributed . The
books shall be open at any and all times for the in-
spection of the superintendent , board of charities
and corrections, the governor , a committee of the
legislature , or any person appointed by either for
examination .

lems . The said university shall be entitled to re-

§ 35. Power of Board ; Management and Control
of Institutions ; Rules ; Sale of Products ; Transfer
of Patients ; Expenditure of Surplus Proceeds .
Said board of charities and corrections shall have
full and complete management and control over all
asylums for the insane and feeble -minded institutes
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , that are now or
may be hereafter established . Said board shall pre-
scribe rules and regulations for the government of
said institutions , for the proper maintenance and
treatment of patients , for the preservation of build-
ings and grounds , for the use and cultivation of
farms and gardens , and the sale of surplus produce ,
the purchase of clothing, food , medicine and all sup-
plies needful to same , and such rules and regula-
tions as prescribed shall be faithfully observed by
all the officers and employees of said institutions ,
and have the power to remove inmates from one
asylum to another . And the said board shall have
power to expend in their discretion any surplus
standing to the credit of said institutions from time
to time , in carrying out the purpose of this act ; and
in so doing they may use such surplus at any of the
institutions under their control without reference
to the fact that such surplus may have arisen at an-
other institution .

rily for poor or infirm persons , to receive or permit
to remain in its shelter a feeble -minded female un-
der forty-five years of age , providing there is room
in the institution for the feeble -minded for such per-
sons , in which case such person must be transferred
to such institution .
§ 49. Duty of Health Officer ; Segregation and
custody to Prevent Procreation . It shall be one of
the special duties of every health officer and every
public health nurse to institute proceedings to se-
cure the proper segration and custody of feeble-
minded persons likely to become fathers or mothers
of other feeble -minded persons .
§ 50. Aiding Marriage of Feeble -Minded Per-
sons , Idiots or Lunatics . It shall be unlawful to aid
or abet the marriage of any feeble -minded person
or to attempt , or to aid or abet an attempt to , the
marriage of an idiot or lunatic and any person
found guilty of a violation of this provision shall be
fined not less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) nor more
than five hundred dollars ($ 500.00 ) .
§ 51. Home ; Farm Colony for Feeble -Minded
Persons . There shall be continued and maintained a
training school for feeble -minded persons , also
maintained a farm colony for them to be located on
land suitable for farming , stock raising and fruit
growing . Such buildings shall be maintained suffi-
cient to accommodate the inmates .
$ 52. Epileptic Persons May be Transferred .
The board is hereby authorized to establish and
maintain a suitable place for epileptic persons and
segregate them from feeble -minded and insane per-
sons , if convenient to do so . It is authorized to trans-
fer said persons to such hospital as is best suited
for the treatment of such cases .

§ 54. Care, Custody and Training . It shall be
the duty of the superintendent of said institution to
provide such care and training for each inmate as

$ 55. Manufacture of Articles . The inmates of
said institution may manufacture and produce for

such articles , furniture , clothing , tools , products and
other supplies and engage in such labor or work of
construction as may be approved by the state board
of charities and corrections .
56. No Person Admitted Under Six Years of
Age . There shall be received into said institution ,
subject to such rules and regulations as the board of
charities and corrections may adopt and pursuant to
the provisions of this act , feeble -minded and epilep-
tic persons of not less than six years of age .

die or cease to be a pauper , a proportionate part of
the allowance from the anniversary date of the al-
lowance to the day of his death or his ceasing to be
a pauper, may be paid .
$ 58. Court May Make Allowance For Pauper
Idiot . No money shall be drawn from the state trea-
sury for the support of an idiot unless the circuit
court wherein the inquest is held shall make the al-
lowance at the time of the inquest .
$ 59. Allowance ; What Persons Not Entitled to .
No allowance shall be made for the maintenance of
any person under the age of eight years , nor for one
who at times , by epileptic fits or other malady , is en-
feebled in mind, or who had not been found to be a
pauper idiot.
$ 60. Inquest to be Held Every Fifth Year.
Every fifth year after the first inquest , before any
order shall be granted by the court for the mainten-
ance of an idiot or a feeble -minded person out of his
own estate , or out of the state treasury , the idiot or
feeble -minded person , in like manner , shall be
brought into the court, or his presence dispensed
with , for the reasons herein allowed ; and the court
shall cause the jury to be empaneled , who shall be
sworn as provided in this article ; and also to in-
quire, and true report to make from the evidence ,
whether the person whom they have in charge has
before been found , by verdict of a jury and judg-
ment of a court , an idiot or a feeble -minded person ,
and whether and what change , if any, has taken
place in his mind , physical condition , and estate
since the previous inquest .

108 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 61. Allowance ; Copy of Inquest to be Furn-
ished Auditor . A copy certified by the clerk of each
original and subsequent inquisition , shall be trans- .
mitted to the auditor of public accounts ; without
such transcript the auditor shall issue no warrant
for the amount allowed by the court .
§ 62. Circuit Clerk to Furnish Annually List of
Pauper Idiots ; Penalty . The circuit court clerk of
each county shall transmit to the auditor , on or be-
fore the tenth of September in each year, a list of
the pauper idiots in his county ; if he fails to do so
without good cause he shall be fined fifty dollars .
63. Definitions . The term " feeble -minded
person " means a person with a defect in mental de-
velopment , which is due to causes operating at birth ,
or at an early age, and which is of such a degree that
he is incapable therefrom of caring for himself or
managing his affairs and requires supervision , care ,
training , control or custody for his own welfare or
for the welfare of others or the community .
The term " epileptic person " means a person who
has epileptic attacks and is known to be losing his
mental capacity as a result of these attacks , and is
so distinctly enfeebled that he needs care and cus-
tody for his own welfare , or for the welfare of
others , or for the community .
The word " idiot " means a person who has been
from birth or infancy deficient in mental capacity ,
and destitute of the ordinary intellectual powers .
The word "lunatic ," or term " insane person "
means one who , having had a mind , has lost it .
§ 64. Drug Addicts . The word "lunatic , " " in-
sane person" or " persons of unsound mind " wher-
ever and whenever used in any law with relation
thereto , or in this act shall be construed to include
any and all persons who by the use of any drug or
narcotic has lost the power of reasoning correctly ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 111
§ 70. Petition for Inquest ; What It Shall State .
The petition , verified as required in the preceding
section , shall set forth the name and residence of the
person concerning whom the petition is filed , the
name , residence of the parent or parents , if known ,
and of the husband or wife , if any , and the name and
residence of the person having custody , control and
supervision of such person ; or if no such person is
known to the petitioner , then of some near relative
or that such is unknown to the petitioner .
§ 71. Court to Appoint Attorney ; Common-
wealth's Attorney . In any case of inquest the court
shall appoint some member of the bar to represent
and protect the interests and rights of the person al-
leged to be of unsound mind , and it shall also be the
duty of the attorney for the commonwealth to pre-
vent the finding of any person to be of unsound mind
who , in his opinion is not such .
$ 72. Notice of Inquest ; Person to be Notified ;
Service of Summons . All persons named in the pe-
tition , including the alleged feeble -minded person ,
epileptic , insane person or idiot , if of legal age , shall
be notified of such proceeding by a summons issued
requiring the parent or parents , guardian or other
person having the custody , control or supervision
of such person or with whom such person may be , to
appear with such person at a time and place to be
stated in the summons , which time shall not be less
than three ( 3 ) days after service . The parents of
such person , if living , if their residence be known to
the petitioner , or the legal guardian , if one there be ,
and his residence is known to the petitioner , or if
there be neither parent or guardian whose residence
is known to the petitioner , then some near relative ,
if his or her residence be known to the petitioner ,
shall be notified also of the proceedings .

When Court May Hold Inquest . If a person sum-
moned as herein provided shall fail without reason-
able cause to appear and abide by the order of the
court, he may be proceeded against as in the case.
of contempt of court . On the return of the summons

§ 74. Trial by Jury , if Desired . In proceeding
under this act the person alleged to be feeble-
minded , epileptic or insane , and appearing before
the court , or any person interested in such person ,
shall have the right to demand for such alleged
feeble -minded , epileptic or insane person a trial by
jury , which shall be granted as in other cases , unless
waived ; or the court on its own motion may call a
jury to try such case . The court in its discretion
may conduct the trial or inquest in chambers .
§ 75. Evidence ; Witnesses May be Summoned .
The court may in its discretion require other evi-
dence in addition to the petition and the certificates
of the examining physicians , and he may require the
attendance at the hearing of said physicians and of
any other witnesses . The alleged feeble -minded , ep-
ileptic , insane person or idiot or any one represent-
ing him shall have the right to summon witnesses
and to present evidence as in other cases made and
provided .

$ 79. Inquest ; Oath to be Administered to Jury ;
Form of ; Instructions of Court . The following oath
shall be administered to the jury : " You do swear
that you will well and truly inquire , and from the
evidence say , in your verdict , whether A B , the per-
son whom you have in charge , is of unsound mind ,
and if of unsound mind , whether he is an idiot or
lunatic that is , whether he was destitute of mind
from infancy or has lost it since birth ; and if he has
lost it since his birth , that you will state when , and
as far as you can from the evidence the cause of it.
You will also inquire and state his birth and resi-
dence , and whether he has been brought into this
state by any person , and by whom , for the purpose
of becoming a charge upon the Commonwealth .
That you will find what estate , the value thereof, he
owns in possession , reversion, or remainder ;
whether his parents are alive ; where they reside ;
and whether they have estate sufficient to support
the person under trial ; whether he is capable of
laboring in whole or in part , and what part , for
his support ." The judge shall instruct the jury
upon the whole case , so as to enable them to' de-
cide the question whether the defendant is an idiot
or lunatic .
But this oath shall not be confused with or in any
wise conflict with the oath to be given upon the in-
quest of an imbecile as prescribed in section 2155 .
§ 80. Pauper ; Who Is ; Verdict ; Orders of

$ 83 . Resident Lunatics and Idiots ; To Which
Asylum To Be Sent . Unless otherwise designated
by the board of charities and corrections , lunatics
and idiots , resident in Kentucky , and found in the

and east of the counties of Hancock, Ohio , Butler ,
Warren and Allen , shall be sent to , and in like man-
ner received by the central asylum for the insane ;
and those found in the counties last named and the
residue of the state shall be sent to and in like man-
ner be received by, the western Kentucky asylum
for the insane .

quest ; Duty of Court . It shall be the duty of the
judge of the court in which any person shall be or-
dered to an institution to at once , by wire or letter ,
notify the superintendent of the institution to which
said person is ordered , that such order has been
made and advise him of the sex and condition of the
person .

auditor of public accounts , and said expenses shall
be paid as provided for in the manner for the pay-
ment of claims . In returning any patient to the
country whence he or she was sent , when necessary ,
the cost of returning them shall be paid in the same
manner . Only one person shall be paid out of the
state treasury for conveying any patient . Each fe-
male committed to any institution shall be accom-
panied by a female attendant , unless accompanied
by her father , brother , husband or son .
86. Information to be Furnished With Com-
mitment . With the commitment of a feeble - minded ,
epileptic , insane person or idiot to an institution for
the feeble -minded , epileptic , insane or idiots , a copy
of the petition , the certificate of the physicians and
the order of commitment , together with a written
statement of the judge and the history of the pa-
tient's case as hereinafter provided shall be sent to
the superintendent of such institution .
§ 87. History of Patient's Case ; Contents of ;
Written Statement ; Duty of Judge at Inquest .
When a person shall be found to be an idiot , feeble-
minded or insane under the provisions of this act
the judge who presides at the inquest shall ascer-
tain and draw up a brief history of such person's
case, embracing the following points :
1. Age ; occupation ; married or single ; habits ;
education . 2. What relations , if any , have been in-
sane, feeble -minded or epileptic . 3. Date of first

$88. Commonwealth or County Attorney : Infor-
mation to Furnish ; Penalty for Failure. The com-
monwealth or county attorney in attendance at the
inquest shall ascertain and furnish to the superin-
tendent information as to the address of the pa-
tient , and also the name of the patient's parents,
their address , and if the party be married , the name
of the husband or wife and his or her address . This
information the said attorney may have the clerk
endorse on the inquest and history of the case to be
forwarded the superintendent .

there is not room in the institution for the person

sought to be committed , the superintendent shall re-

eptic , insane person or idiot to guardianship until

the judge shall send to the superintendent a copy of

ianship and a copy of the original order of commit-

$ 90. Occurrence of Vacancy ; Disposition of
Patient ; Termination of Guardianship ; Duty of
Superintendent to Give Notice and Send for Pa-
tient ; Allowance Prorated . When a vacancy occurrs
in an institution to which a court has sought to com-
mit a patient but because , through lack of accommo-
dation at said institution for such patients , the pa-
tient was committed to guardianship , the superin-
tendent shall immediately notify the judge and clerk
of the court having charge of the patient and shall
send an attendant to convey such patient to the in-
stitution . Such person shall be delivered to the at-
tendant immediately upon arrival and the commit-
ment to guardianship shall thereupon cease and the
original order of commitment to the institution
shall take effect .
$ 91. Pauper Idiot Not to Be Sent to Asylum
Unless Dangerous : Verdict at Inquest to Show . No
order shall be made by any court or officer for send-
ing a pauper idiot to an asylum , nor shall such idiot
be received therein unless the jury by their verdict
on the inquest , shall find that he is so dangerous or
uncontrollable that he can not be safely and prop-
erly kept by a committee within the county .

§ 92. Refusal to Receive . The superintendent
or person in charge of any institution for the feeble-
minded or insane may refuse to receive any person
upon an order of commitment , if the papers pre-
sented do not comply with the provisions of this
act, or if in his judgment the person is not feeble-
minded or insane within the meaning of this act .
The attendant sent for a patient shall examine the
papers before he conveys the patient to the institu-
tion and shall not convey the patient if his papers
are incomplete , but his approval of them shall not
be conclusive .
§ 93. Conveyance to Asylum ; When County to
Pay ; Mileage Allowed and When Not Paid ; Orders
of Court . If the court wherein an inquest is held
shall order as necessary an additional guard his ex-
penses shall be paid by the county and the costs
shall not exceed six cents per mile for the guard or
guards and patient , each , going , and six cents per
mile for the guard returning , the distance to be es-
timated by the nearest usual route of travel . If the
patient can not be received by the asylum , six cents
per mile for his returning shall also be allowed ; but
no allowance for expenses of either guard or pa-
tient to or from the asylum shall be made unless
either an application has been made first by letter
to the superintendent or an order of court made to
carry the patient to the asylum immediately on ac-
count of violent and dangerous character .
§ 94. Idiots , Epileptics and Harmless Lunatics ;
Disposition of . All pauper idiots , epileptics and
harmless , incurable lunatics shall be returned by the
asylum in which they may be confined to the several
counties from which they are sent and delivered
into the custody of their friends , if any ; if not , then
to the county judge , or , if they be residents of and
sent from the city of Louisville , then to the mayor

95. Restoration ; Inquest ; Discharge as Cured .
No person who has been adjudged of unsound mind
at an inquest shall be discharged from any institu-
tion whatever as cured or restored until he has been
found, by an inquest for restoration held in the
court which adjudged him of unsound mind , to be
cured or restored .
§ 96. Inquiry as to Restoration ; Verdict ; Judg-
ment . Whenever it shall appear , from an affidavit
filed , to the county or circuit court in which any per-
son has been adjudged of unsound mind, that a per-
son of unsound mind has been restored to his pro-
per senses, or that the inquest was false or fraudu-
lent the court shall forthwith direct the facts to be
inquired into by a jury in open court and make all
necessary orders and decrees in the premises .
If the jury shall find the person charged who had
heretofore been found of unsound mind , to be of
sound mind then the court shall enter an order and
judgment that said person is restored and give him
all the rights and privileges of a person of sound
mind.
§ 97. Duty of Clerk of Court ; Penalty . When
an inquest of restoration is held and the person
upon whom the inquest is held is adjudged to be of
sound mind and is restored , the clerk of the court
shall locate among his records the order adjudging
such person a person of unsound mind and the in-
dex thereof and shall note said restoration thereon

§ 101. Appeal to Circuit Court . Circuit Courts
shall have the jurisdiction in all cases for the restor-
ation of persons to sound mind , and any party in
interest shall have the right to appeal from the
judgment of the County Court to the Circuit Court ,
and upon the appeal the Circuit Court shall hear the
proceedings de novo .
§ 102. Unlawful Inquest ; Penalty . Whoever
shall knowingly conspire or contrive to have one
unlawfully or fraudulently adjudged an idiot or
feeble -minded or insane , or confined in any institu-
tion whatever within the boundaries of this state
for the feeble -minded , epileptic or insane , shall be
fined not less than $ 100 nor more than $ 500 , or im-
prisoned not less than three months nor more than
one year , or both .
103. Penalty for Bringing Persons Into This
State to be a Charge Upon the State or Fraudu-
lently Procuring an Adjudication as an Idiot . Who-
ever shall bring or cause to be brought into any
county or city of this commonwealth from any state
or country any feeble -minded , epileptic, insane per-
son or idiot with the intent to make him a charge
upon this commonwealth , or who shall fraudulently
secure any adjudication that a person is a pauper
idiot with the purpose of securing the allowance
provided by this act , shall be fined $ 100 or impris-
oned three months or both , and shall further be li-
able , at the suit of the commonwealth , for all dam-
ages incurred thereby , including the cost of trans-
portation .

board with not more than five ( 5 ) members from

bers shall be women . The members of the bureau

appointments , except to fill vacancies caused by
death , resignation or removal , shall be for a full
term of three years , and until their successors are
appointed and qualified .

CHAPTER 18 .
AN ACT for the establishment and maintenance at each of the
State Hospitals for the Insane in the State of Kentucky a
training school for psychiatric nursing for attendants , pre-
scribing the course of study to be required in said school , the
method of appointments of attendants , the qualifications re-
quired of such attendants , fixing the salaries of such attend-
ants , and for the employment of a supervisor of such training
school and the qualifications required of such supervisor , pre-
scribing the duties of such supervisor and fixing the salary
for such position .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That on or before September first , one thousand
nine hundred and twenty -eight , the State Board of
Charities and Corrections shall establish and main-
tain at each of the State Hospitals for the Insane in
the State of Kentucky a training school for psy-
chiatric nursing for attendants . The course of
study in said schools shall consist of two years work
and shall include the following studies : First , nurs-
ing mental and nervous cases ; second , elementary
nursing ; third , psychiatry and applied psychology ;
fourth , public sanitation ; fifth , elementary dietet-
ics ; sixth , occupational theropy ; seventh , history
and the ethics of nursing ; eighth , personal hygiene ;
ninth , .elementary principles of massage ; tenth ,
recreational theropy ; eleventh , hospital etiquette
and all such practical work as the superintendents
of the State Hospital shall direct . The course of
study and regulations shall be arranged and estab-
lished by the superintendents of the State Hospi-

146 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there is hereby established The Judi-
cial Council of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Such Council shall consist of the Judges of the
Court of Appeals of Kentucky and the Circuit
Judges of said Commonwealth . The Chief Justice
of the Court of Appeals shall be the presiding offi-
cer .
§ 2. Said Council shall meet on the call of the
Chief Justice of the Court of Appeals . Said meet-
ing shall be called at least once a year at some con-
venient time , and at some convenient place in the
State . It shall be the duty of all members of said
Council to attend such Session .
§ 3. It shall be the duty of said Judicial Coun-
cil to study the organization , rules , methods of
procedure , and practice of the judicial system of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the work accom-
plished and the results produced by that system in
its various parts ; the problems of administration
confronting the Courts of the Commonwealth and
the judicial system in general . It shall be the duty
of each Circuit Judge to prepare and submit to the
Council at such sessions a report setting for the
conditions in the Circuit Courts over which he pre-
sides and of the business dispatched and pending
in said Circuit Courts . It shall be the duty of said
Council to report biennially to the general Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky concerning
the work of the various branches of the judicial
system of the Commonwealth together with any
recommendations it may have for the modification
or amelioration of existing conditions or for any
amendments to the Codes of Practice and pro-
cedure .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 15 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky , approved March 24 , 1926 ,
which was , "An Act to repeal , amend and re- enact
Section 108 of the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition , 1915 , be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended and re -enacted so that when amended the
said section shall read as follows :
" No partnership for the purpose of practicing
law shall exist between the clerk of any Circuit
Court and the clerk of any County Court , or any
judge of any court, or between either of said offi-
cers and any attorney - at -law , except that in cities
of the third , fourth , fifth and sixth class , the Judge
of the Police Court may be permitted to form a
partnership for the practice of law with an attor-
ney - at-law , but a partner of said Police Judge shall .
not be permitted to practice law in any case pend-
ing in the court presided over by said officer . Any
person violating any of the provisions of the pre-
ceding Section shall be guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction shall be fined not less than
$25.00 , or more than $500.00 , for each offense . "
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Approved March 21 , 1928 .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 161
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That all Circuit Courts of this Common-
wealth , sitting in any county containing a city of
the second or third class , which are not now Courts
of continuous sessions , shall be , on and after Janu-
ary 1 , 1929 , Courts of continuous sessions , for the
trial of cases on the Equity Docket , and for the trial
of all cases on the Common Law Docket , except
where trial by jury is called for or ordered by the
court , provided , however , that no order or judgment
shall be made or taken in any case , except on rea-
sonable notice to all parties in interest , or their at-
torneys, or by agreement .
§ 2. That all regular terms of court in all such
Circuit Courts shall be held as now provided by
law, or as may hereafter be provided by law.
§ 3. That all laws of this Commonwealth now
governing Courts of continuous sessions shall gov-
ern all such Circuit Courts as to all cases on the
Equity Docket , or on the Common Law Docket , ex-
cept where trials by jury are called for or ordered
by the court as herein provided , and such trials
shall be held during the term provided by law .
§ 4. That all laws or parts of laws in conflict
herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved March 20 , 1928 .

and section 2554a -43 , Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Supple-

only of the conflict between this act and of said sections .

The drawer of any such check , draft or order

is delivered ; provided , no indictment shall be made
under the provisions of this Act within ten days
after the date of such check , draft or order . The
place of delivery shall be construed to mean the

Provided , however , that if the person who makes ,
issues , utters or delivers any such check , draft or
order , shall pay the same within ten days from the
time he receives actual notice , written or verbal of
the dishonor of such check , draft or order , then any
prosecution that may have been instituted within
said time , shall , if payment be made on such check
as aforesaid , be dismissed at defendant's cost . A
written notice from the bank or institution on which
said check , draft or order is drawn or from a holder,
thereof addressed to the drawer at the last known
place of residence shall be prima facie evidence of
notice of such dishonor .
Provided , however , that past ( post ) dated checks ,
drafts or orders shall be subject to the provisions of
this Act and shall not escape the penalties hereof,
and provided further that nothing in this Act shall
apply to any person or persons under twenty - one
years of age .

CHAPTER 42.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 1157a , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's edition , Chapter 36 , subdivision 3 , of date
March 21 , 1902 , relating to kidnapping and holding persons for
ransom .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 1157a , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition , Chapter 36 , sub -division 3, of date March
21 , 1902 , be repealed and re -enacted , and when so
repealed and re-enacted shall read as follows :
Any person or persons who forcibly or otherwise
hold any person against his or her will for the pur-
pose of unlawfully obtaining a ransom for the re-
lease of such person , or who may aid or abet any
person thus offending , shall , on conviction thereof ,
be deemed guilty of a felony and punished by con-
finement in the State penitentiary for life or by
death , in the discretion of the jury .
Any person or persons charged with such offense
may be tried in any county into or through which
the person or child so seized , inveigled , decoyed ,
kidnapped , abducted or otherwise taken shall have
been carried or brought .
Any act or part of an act in conflict herewith is
hereby repealed .
Approved March 21 , 1928 .

Be it enacted of the General Assembly of the

§ 1. There shall be submitted to the qualified

the Constitution and the laws of this Common-
wealth an amendment to the Constitution of Ken-
tucky which shall be known and designated as Sec-

Section 246a . The provisions of Section 246 of
the Constitution of Kentucky shall not apply to

enacted pursuant thereto .

scribed by the Constitution and laws of this State ,
the Secretary of State shall cause the question to be
submitted in the following form to-wit :
" Shall the provisions of Section 246 of the Con-
stitution apply to Judges of the Court of Appeals
including those now (in ) office ?"

fishes with pole and line , hand line , set line , trot line
or throw line in this State without first obtaining a

nor more than fifty dollars , but any resident owner ,
landlord or tenant , and members of their families
may fish in the waters of their own lands or lease-

such lands or leaseholds , in season without obtain-

sary to carry out the provisions of this act and of
the game laws shall be printed under the direction
of the Game and Fish Commission , and shall be
paid for in like manner and upon the same terms as
other public printing . This expense shall be charged
to the Game and Fish Protection Fund .

CHAPTER 74 .
AN ACT enabling cities of the first class to construct , operate
and maintain bridges across navigable streams , so as to con-
nect such cities with an adjoining State ; providing for a com-
mission in such cities of the first class to construct , operate ,
control and maintain such bridges and fixing the powers and
duties of such commission and providing for the issuance of
revenue bonds of such cities to pay the cost thereof payable
solely through bridge tolls without the incurrence of indebted-
ness of any such city.

§ 1. Definitions . The word " bridge " whenever
used in this act , shall include the actual bridge be-

thereto , including the substructures and the super-

equipment thereof ; the word " approaches , " when-
ever used in this act , shall be construed to mean all
that portion of the bridge extending from the begin-
ning of the approach to the furthermost abutment
of the bridge on the same side of the river , but not
to include such abutment . The word " super -struc-
ture , " whenever used in this act , shall be construed
to mean all that portion of the bridge between the
approaches . The term " substructure ," whenever
used in this act , shall include all that portion of the
bridge not included within the meaning of the defini-
tion of super -structure or the approaches , and shall
include the piers and abutments . The terms " cost of

constructing the super -structure and substructure

CHAPTER 75 .
AN ACT creating the office of purchasing agent in counties con-
taining cities of the first class , defining his duties and fixing
his salary.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in counties containing a city of the
first class the office of Purchasing Agent is hereby
created .
§ 2. Such Purchasing Agent shall be elected by
the Fiscal Court of such county for a term of four
years . The first Purchasing Agent shall be elected
at a meeting of the Fiscal Court succeeding the
passage of this Act , for a term of four years , from
the first day of May , 1928 , and every four years
thereafter .
§ 3. It shall be the duty of such Purchasing
Agent to make all purchases of supplies , goods ,
wares and merchandise of all description needed
by such county containing a city of the first class ,
and for all departments of the county government
of such county , where the amount of purchases shall
not exceed the sum of two hundred fifty dollars
($250.00 ) .
For purchases where the amount exceeds two
hundred and fifty ($250.00 ) dollars said purchases

CHAPTER 76 .
AN ACT to amend Section 2948a -1, Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
edition of 1922 , relating to municipal universities in cities of
the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2948a - 1 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's Edition of 1922 , be , and is hereby amended
by inserting in line 6 in lieu of the words and figure
" one cent ( 1c ) , " the words and figures " five cents
(5c) , " and by inserting in line 7 in lieu of the words
and figure " five cents (5c ) , " the words and figure
" seven cents ( 7c ) ," so that said Section , as
amended , shall read as follows :
Section 2948a - 1 . That the general council of any
city of the first class , which has a municipal univer-
sity as defined in this Act , may annually cause to be
levied and collected for support of such university
a tax of not less than five cents ( 5c ) nor more than
seven cents (7c) on each one hundred dollars
($100.00 ) worth of property subject to taxation for
city purposes .
Approved March 21, 1928 .

CHAPTER 77 .
AN ACT providing for the creation and maintenance in counties
having therein a city of the first class of a board with power
to establish and maintain in such counties airports , aero-
dromes and aircraft fields , and defining the powers of the
board , and authorizing the levy and collection of taxes to
raise funds for the purposes of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby created in each county of
the Commonwealth in which there is a city of the
first class a by-partisan board composed of six
members , which is a body politic and shall be known
as the (name of city ) and . (name
of county ) Air Board , and in such name said board
may sue and be sued , contract and be contracted
with , acquire real and personal property by pur-
chase , lease , gift, condemnation or otherwise , and
may do all things reasonable or necessary to effec-
tively carry out the work and properly perform the
duties intended or required by this act .
The members of the board shall be appointed
jointly by the mayor of such city and the judge of
the county court of such county . One -half of the
members of the board shall be of one political
party , and the other half of the other political party
which at the last preceding general election in such
county respectively cast the highest and the next
highest number of votes . Members of said board
shall serve for terms of ( 4 ) years each , and until
their successors are appointed and qualified . Such
members shall be bona fide residents of such county .
§ 2. Members of the said board shall serve
without compensation but shall be allowed any rea-
sonable expenses incurred by them in the conduct
of the affairs of said board . It shall, upon the ap-
pointment of its members , organize and elect offi-

CHAPTER 80 .
AN ACT to provide for the creation and organization of a city
planning and zoning commission for cities of the second class
and surrounding territory which bears relation to the planning
and zoning thereof ; to prescribe and regulate the powers and
duties of such commission ; to empower cities of the second
class , through the mayor , board of commissioners or general
council thereof, to take such action as may be necessary , by
ordinance or otherwise , to approve and effectuate the commis-
sion's action insofar as it may affect property within the limits
of such city, and to provide a fund for defraying the expenses
of the commission and to levy a tax for that purpose ; to
authorize the fiscal court of the county in which a city of the
second class is located , to approve or disapprove the action
of the commission insofar as it affects property located out-
side the limits of such city , but within the limits of the
county ; to empower cities of the second class , by ordinance ,
to provide for the appointment of a board of adjustment and
define the powers and prescribe the procedure of such board ;
to provide for an appeal to the circuit court of the county ,
and thence to the Court of Appeals , from the orders , decisions
or rulings of the board of adjustment ; to provide penalties
for violation of this act or any ordinance or regulation made
under authority conferred thereby , and also to provide a
remedy by proceedings in court for an injunction or other
similar relief , repealing sections 2741h , 2741h -1, 2741h -2,
2741h -3, 2741h -4 of the Kentucky Statutes , relating to city
planning and zoning in cities of the second class .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 295
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby created for each city of the
second class and surrounding territory which bears
relation to the planning or zoning thereof , a commis-
sion by the name of " City Planning and Zoning
Commission," which Commission shall be consti-
tuted as hereinafter provided , and shall have the
powers , duties and functions hereinafter conferred
or imposed upon it .
CREATION OF COMMISSION .
§ 2. Each Commission shall consist of seven
members , five of whom shall be known as " city mem-
bers " and shall be citizens and residents of the city
affected thereby , and two of whom shall be known
as " county members " and shall be citizens of the
county in which such city is located , residing outside
the limits of such city .
The city members shall be chosen in the following
manner : The mayor and supervisor of public works
shall be ex-officio members of the Commission. The
mayor shall appoint the remaining three city mem-
bers who shall be qualified persons holding no other
city or county office .
The county members shall be chosen in the follow-
ing manner : The county engineer shall be ex-officio
member of the Commission . The remaining member
shall be chosen by the fiscal court from qualified per-
sons not holding any other city or county office .
All members of said Commission shall serve with-
out compensation .
The members initially appointed by the mayor
shall hold their offices , according to designation in
their appoinment , one of them for a term of two
years, and the others for terms of four years each ,
and thereafter , upon the expiration of their re-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 335
CHAPTER 86 .
AN ACT to amend Section 3414 , Kentucky Statutes relating to
the qualifications of city collectors in third class cities , and
consolidating the offices of city treasurer and city collector .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 3414 of Chapter 89 , Kentucky Stat-
utes ( Carroll ) , be and the same is hereby amended
by striking out the word " male " in the second line
thereof and adding at the end of said section the
words " the city council may by ordinance consoli-
date the offices of City Collector and City Treasurer
under the name City Collector - Treasurer , and the
same shall thereafter constitute but one office . " So
that , when amended , said section shall read as fol-
lows :
"Section 3414.-Qualification ; Re -election .- No
one shall be eligible to the office of Collector of
taxes unless he be a citizen of the city , at least 21
years of age and a bona fide resident of said city ;
but no collector of taxes shall be eligible to re-ap-
pointment or re- election to the same office until he
shall have made a settlement with the Council of all
his accounts and paid over all moneys in his hands ,
and have obtained from said Council a quietus . The
City Council may by ordinance consolidate the of-
fices of City Collector and City Treasurer under the
name City Collector - Treasurer , and the same shall
hereafter constitute but one office ."
Approved March 21 , 1928 .

may sue and be sued , but the private property of

and convey the same , to have and to exercise all

sary , and proper , and expedient , and wise , to adver-
tise , promote , advance , and establish and develop
the resources and advantages of said city , as a busi-
ness and industrial center .
§ 4. No Recording Fee .-The articles of incor-
poration shall be recorded by the county clerk with-
out fee of any kind .
•§ 5. No Salaries to Officers or Directors .— No
salary shall be paid to any officer , except the board
of directors may employ a general secretary , whose
salary shall be fixed before his employment .
6. No Dividends .-No dividend shall ever be
declared by this corporation . Any division of funds
or profits of such corporation to any purpose except
the purposes of the corporation shall constitute a
larceny , and be punished as provided by law .
7. Treasurer's Bond .-The treasurer and the
secretary shall give bond , the amount to be fixed by
the directors and in no case less than the sum likely
to come within their hands .

350 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 90 .
AN ACT authorizing and empowering cities of the fourth class
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , to purchase or receive by
way of donation , lands or property for public parks or play-
grounds , and authorizing governing bodies of said cities to
levy taxes to maintain same , and the appointment of a park
board and their duties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That all cities of the fourth class in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , shall have power and
authority to purchase lands or property for the pur-
pose of maintaining public parks or playgrounds
within the city limits of said cities , or to receive
donations of lands or property within the city limits
for public parks and playground purposes .
§ 2. Said cities of the fourth class are hereby
authorized and empowered to levy taxes not exceed-
ing five cents on each one hundred dollars on all
taxable property within the corporate limits of said
city , to purchase and maintain public parks or play-
grounds within the limits of said cities of the fourth
class .
3. Be it further enacted that the city councils
or other governing bodies of said cities of the fourth
class be and they are hereby authorized to appoint
a Park Board of not more than five persons , whose
qualifications shall be such as may be prescribed by
council , and whose term of office shall be for two
years and until their successors are appointed and
qualified , except that the members first appointed
upon the taking effect of this act shall be appointed
as follows : Three members for one year , and two
members for two years , and thereafter whenever a
vacancy occurs . The members of said Park Board
shall not receive any salary for their services .
§ 4. The persons so appointed as members of
said Park Board shall each within ten days after

classification.reasoning: The statute does not mention race or impose any restrictions based on race. It focuses on the establishment and maintenance of public parks and playgrounds, which are not inherently discriminatory. Therefore, it is classified as 'no' for Jim Crow laws.

CHAPTER 92 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale , lease or disposal of any system of
water works or electric lighting system in cities of the fourth
class where such city owns or is engaged in operating a sys-
tem of water works or electric lighting system , without the
assent of the majority of voters of said city voting therefor
at an election for the purpose , and providing for such an elec-
tion.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. That no city of the fourth class which now
owns or is engaged in operating a system of water
works or electric lighting system shall sell , lease or
otherwise dispose of the same without the assent of
two -thirds of the total number of legal voters of
such city voting at an election held for that purpose ,
at a regular November election .
§ 2. Before any such an election shall be held
the city council of said city shall , not less than ninety
days before the next regular November election ,
duly and regularly pass an ordinance which shall
state all of the provisions and terms upon which
such system of waterworks , or electric lighting sys-
tem for furnishing its inhabitants with water or elec-
tric current , shall be sold , and submit the question
of the sale of said system of said waterworks or elec-
tric lighting system to the voters of said city at the
next regular November election .

CHAPTER 95 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 3096 of the Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's edition 1922 , relating to improvement of
public ways , costs , penalty , assessment of street railway and
street intersections and relating to the apportionment of said
cost of said construction upon abutting property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 3096 of the Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1922 edition , be amended and re-enacted
by making certain corrections of typographical
errors therein and by adding thereto subsections to
be designated as subsections 2 and 3 thereof , and
which subsections shall read as follows :
Subsection 2. All assessments theretofore made
against the abutting property for the construction
of streets , sidewalks , curbs , gutters and sewers shall
be taken together in the aggregate in computing
whether or not any particular assessment for the
improvement of such property , which is apportioned
against the abutting property owners , exceeds one-
half of the value of the lot or parcel of real estate
upon which the assessment is made as herein before
set out , and the total of all street and sewer improve-
ments assessed against such abutting property shall
not exceed one-half of the value of the real estate or
lot upon and against which the assessment is made ,
and such assessment shall be void as to such excess ,
as hereinbefore provided .
Subsection 3. All laws and parts of laws in
conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
So that section 3096 Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1922 edition , when so amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows :
Section 3096. The general council may, by ordi-
nance , provide for the improvement of the streets ,

and burial grounds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That whenever any " Cemetery , Graveyard
or Burial Grounds " lie within the corporate limits
of any city of this Commonwealth , whether first ,
second , third , fourth , fifth or sixth class city , it shall
become the duty of the governing authorities of
such city or cities , to protect said " Cemetery ,
Graveyard , or Burial Grounds " from being used
for dumping grounds , building sites , play grounds ,
places of entertainment and amusement , public
parks , athletic fields or parking grounds for ve-
hicles and automobiles .

or Burial Grounds , " lying within the corporate
limits of any city of this Commonwealth , whether
first , second , third , fourth , fifth or sixth class city,
is owned by a corporation , individual or individ-
uals , church or heirs of donors or has reverted to
heirs or donors , the governing authorities of said
city or cities may require said corporation , individ-
ual or individuals , church , heirs of donors or those
having claims to said grounds , to properly care for
same .

CHAPTER 104 .
AN ACT appropriating five thousand dollars to be used toward
the creation of a memorial park in commemoration of the
Battle of Belmont , the Columbus Military Encampment and in
honor of the Union and Confederate soldiers of Kentucky who
engaged in said battle and occupied said encampment , said
park to be known and designated as "The Columbus -Belmont
Battlefield Park ," providing that said five thousand dollars
shall not be withdrawn from the treasury until twenty thou-
sand dollars has been donated in cash by popular subscrip-
tion , and a deed in fee simple title , free from all encumbrance ,
has been executed and delivered to the Kentucky State Park
Commission for about one -hundred and ten acres of land lo-
cated in and near Columbus , Hickman County , Kentucky , and
describing said one hundred and ten acres by meets and
bounds , the title thereto to be examined and approved by the
Attorney General of Kentucky , and providing that when said
conditions have been complied with it shall be the duty of the
Kentucky State Park Commission to at once establish and
maintain a State park upon and within said land for the bene-
fit and enjoyment of the people of Kentucky , and providing
that said Kentucky State Park Commission shall then with-
draw from the State Treasury said five thousand dollars ap-
propriated and use said sum , together with the twenty thou-
sand dollars donated , in fencing and beautifying said park and
in identifying with permanent markers and for preserving the
trenches and embankments of the Columbus Military Encamp-
ment , preserving the remaining relics of said battle and en-
campment , and such military and historic points of interest
connected with said battle and encampment , providing for
permission to construct monuments in said park by military ,
historic or patriotic associations commemorating the valor ,
devotion and heroism of any American military organization
or soldiers , with the permission of the Kentucky State Park
Commission .

dollars is hereby appropriated out of any funds in
the treasury of Kentucky not otherwise appropri-
ated to be used toward the creation of a memorial
park in commemoration of the Battle of Belmont ,

General George Rogers Clark Memorial Commission , defining

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there is hereby created a commission
to be known as the George Rogers Clark Memorial
Commission , which shall be composed of five per-
sons , citizens of the State of Kentucky . Said Com-
missioners shall be appointed by the Governor of
the Commonwealth and shall be residents of the
State of Kentucky for not less than four years , next
preceding of the adoption of this act . Three of said
commissioners shall hold office for two years and two
for four years from the date of their first appoint-
ment by the Governor . After which all of said com-
missioners shall hold for a period of four years , but
the term of two of them shall expire by biennially .
Any and all vacancies occurring in said commission ,
shall be filled for the unexpired term by the Gover-
nor . Said commissioners , or any of them , may be
removed at any time for cause .
§ 2. The members of said commission and their
successors in office , shall be and are hereby consti-
tuted a body corporate with all the powers necessary
to carry into effect all the purposes of this act . Said
commissioners after their appointment and quali-
fications , shall adopt a seal and organize by electing
from their membership a president, secretary and
treasurer , to serve for the period of two years , or
until their successor is elected and qualified , but the
same person may be elected to serve both as secre-
tary and treasurer of the commission , but said sec-
retary and treasurer , whether or not the office be
united in one person , shall execute a bond to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the faithful per-

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That chapter twenty -one of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred and
eight be and the same is hereby amended and re-
enacted so that the same when so amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows : Section One . Pay
of Officers of Election .- Officers of election shall re-
ceive pay as follows : Judges , three dollars each ;
sheriffs , three dollars each ; clerks , three dollars
each . In all elections to fill vacancies , the same fees .
For delivering election returns the sheriff and judge ,
whose duty it is to accompany him , shall also receive
eight cents for each mile necessarily traveled from
the place of voting to and from the place of delivery .
The fiscal court shall, at its regular October term ,
provide for the payment of all election officers of the
ensuing November election , which payment shall be
made out of the levy of the year in which the services
are rendered , or out of any money in the hands of
the treasurer or sheriff not otherwise appropriated .
2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Approved March 17 , 1928 .

power to appoint for his department the assistants
and employes hereinafter enumerated , who shall
serve for the term for which he is elected , unless
sooner removed by him . They shall perform such
duties , not otherwise provided by law, as may be
assigned them by the attorney general , as legal ad-
visers for the several officers , departments and insti-
tutions of the Commonwealth , or in furtherance of
the discharge of his duties .
There shall be a first assistant attorney general ,
who , in case of the absence or sickness of the at-
torney general, shall perform the duties of the
latter , and said first assistant attorney general shall
be more than thirty years of age and shall have been
a regular practicing attorney for eight years , and
his salary shall be four thousand dollars per an-
num , payable monthly out of the State Treasury .
There shall be a second assistant attorney gen-
eral , who shall have the same qualifications as the
first assistant attorney general and receive the same
salary , payable in the same way .
There shall be a third assistant attorney general ,
who shall be over thirty years of age , and shall have
been a regular practicing attorney for six years , who
shall be paid a salary of four thousand dollars per
annum , payable monthly out of the State Treasury .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the following words and figures , fifteen hun-
dred dollars , be stricken from section four thousand
six hundred and eighty -three of the Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's edition , and being a portion of Ses-
sion Acts of one thousand eight hundred and ninety-
three , and inserting in lieu thereof the words twenty-
five hundred dollars , so that when said section is
amended it shall read as follows : " Section four
thousand six hundred and eighty -three . The treas-
urer , shall , during the term of the present in-
cumbent , receive the salary and perquisites now al-
lowed by law , and , shall thereafter receive an annual
salary of thirty-six hundred dollars , in lieu of all
fees and other compensation , payable in the same
manner as the salary of the Governor . The assist-
ant shall receive an annual salary of twenty -five
hundred dollars , payable in the same manner as the
salary of the Governor ."
Section 2. Whereas , the salary of fifteen hundred
dollars for assistant treasurer was fixed in one thou-
sand eight hundred and ninety-three when living ex-
penses were low and salaries meager , and since said.
time living expenses have increased to such an ex-
tent that sufficient and proper trained person can
not surrender his business and take upon himself
the duties of assistant treasurer for such meager
salary . Therefore , an emergency is declared to
exist , and this act shall take effect upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 20 , 1928 .

AN ACT to provide for the establishment and maintenance in

for the treatment of persons afflicted with tuberculosis .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In each county of the Commonwealth in
which there is a city of the first class there shall be
created a board to be composed of twelve members ,
which board shall be a body politic and corporate
and shall be known as the " Board of Tuber-
culosis Hospital , " and in such corporate name
said board may sue and be sued , contract and
be contracted with , acquire real and personal
property by gift, purchase and condemnation ,
and may do all other things which may be rea-
sonable and necessary to effectively carry out the
work , and to properly perform the duties intended
or required by this act . Ten of the members of said
board shall be appointive and the mayor of said city
shall ex-officio be a member thereof and the county
judge of said county shall ex-officio be a member
thereof . Provided , however , that from and after
the appointment and qualification of the director of
health in such city , the mayor of said city shall cease
to be ex-officio a member of said board , and in his
stead the director of health in said city shall from
and after said time be ex -officio a member thereof .
The appointive members of said board shall be ap-
pointed jointly by the mayor of such city, and by
the judge of the County Court of such county . Pro-
